heading: Taminick vs Tamleugh North — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Taminick, Victoria and Tamleugh North, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Taminick and Tamleugh North have similar overall climate risk scores.

faq.question: Is Taminick or Tamleugh North safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Taminick has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Tamleugh North scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Taminick scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Tamleugh North scores 39/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Taminick or Tamleugh North?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Taminick has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
